data.title: Bitmine has staked another 79,296 ETH, bringing its total staked amount to 154,176 ETH.
data.description: Onchain Lens monitoring shows that Bitmine has staked another 79,296 ETH, worth $232 million. Its total staked ETH is 154,176, worth $451 million. Previously, it was reported that Bitmine staked 74,880 ETH, worth $219 million, early this morning.

data.title: Russian traffic police officer sentenced to 7 years in prison for stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of Bitcoin through violent evidence collection.
data.description: A traffic police officer in Ufa, Russia, has been sentenced to seven years in prison for stealing approximately 20 million rubles (about hundreds of thousands of US dollars) worth of Bitcoin. The case dates back to 2022, during which the officer illegally obtained access to the mobile phones of two detainees, transferring BTC through messaging apps and cryptocurrency wallets, and also used violence during the investigation. The court sentenced him to seven years in a regular prison, ordered him to pay approximately 20 million rubles in compensation to the victims, and stripped him of his police rank. (Bits.media)

data.title: Predictive AI Takes on Prediction Markets
data.description:
Synthdata's decentralized network for financial AI gets to work on Polymarket.
source: https://www.bankless.com/read/testing-predictive-ai-in-prediction-markets

data.title: Former Russian Traffic Officer Sentenced for Bitcoin Theft
data.description: According to BlockBeats, a former traffic police officer in Ufa, Russia, has been sentenced to seven years in prison for stealing approximately 20 million rubles worth of Bitcoin. The incident dates back to 2022 when the officer illegally accessed the mobile phones of two detained individuals during an investigation. He used messaging apps and crypto wallets to transfer Bitcoin, employing violent methods to obtain evidence. The court has sentenced him to a regular correctional facility for seven years, ordered him to compensate the victims with around 20 million rubles, and stripped him of his police rank.

data.title: Yuga Labs announced its acquisition of Improbable, the Unreal Engine creation platform, and will absorb its development team.
data.description: Garga.eth, co-founder of BAYC, announced on the X platform that Yuga Labs has acquired Improbable, the Unreal Engine creation platform, and entered into a perpetual licensing agreement with Improbable for high-concurrency technology for Otherside. In addition, engineers and developers from the platform will also join Yuga Labs. The specific acquisition amount has not yet been disclosed. It is understood that Yuga Labs plans to further expand Improbable's software and tools in 2026 and accelerate the delivery of new Otherside game experiences.

data.title: CoreWeave chief flags extreme supply chain stress as AI growth hits deeper bottlenecks
data.description:
CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator said at the Fortune Brainstorm AI conference in San Francisco Friday that the so called circular AI economy label does not match what is happening inside the industry. Michael rejected claims that the current AI boom is driven by money looping between the same companies rather than real demand. Michael said […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/coreweave-flags-extreme-supply-chain-stress/

data.title: Peter Brandt: Silver Still Far From Overbought as Quarterly Trend Expands
data.description:
A new chart analysis shared by Peter Brandt focuses on silver’s long-term structure using quarterly data, with particular attention on the Average Directional Index (ADX). The commentary frames silver as being well removed from overbought conditions, despite the recent expansion in price.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/peter-brandt-silver-still-far-from-overbought-as-quarterly-trend-expands/

data.title: JPMorgan Excludes Crypto Stocks From 2026 Stock Recommendations
data.description:
JPMorgan's 2026 stock list excludes crypto-related concept stocks, sparking industry-wide reactions.
source: https://coincu.com/news/jpmorgan-2026-stock-list-crypto-exclusion/

data.title: Binance: ARB Chain Alpha Trading Competition rewards began being distributed at 11:00 AM today.
data.description: Binance officially announced on X that the rewards for the ARB Chain Alpha Trading Competition (including VSN and LAVA) began to be distributed at 11:00 on December 27. Users who claim their rewards 48 hours later will receive an additional 5 Binance Alpha Points (either VSN or LAVA must meet the rules).

data.title: LayerZero Protocol Fee Switch Remains Inactive After Vote
data.description: According to Odaily, the LayerZero Foundation announced on X that the third vote regarding the activation of the 'fee switch' has concluded. Due to insufficient voter turnout, the fee switch for the LayerZero protocol will remain inactive. The next voting session is scheduled to take place in six months.

data.title: Jan3 CEO: 2025 will be a bear market, but Bitcoin will enter a long-term bull market that lasts until 2035.
data.description: Jan3 CEO Samson Mow stated on the X platform that 2025 will be a bear market, but Bitcoin may be about to enter a bull market that lasts until 2035.

data.title: LayerZero's third vote on the "fee switch" failed to reach the quorum requirement and will remain closed.
data.description: The LayerZero Foundation officially announced on X that the third vote on opening the "fee switch" has concluded. Due to insufficient quorum, LayerZero's "fee switch" will remain closed. The next vote will be held in six months.

data.title: An address suspected of being linked to the YGG vault deposited 40 million YGG tokens, worth $2.65 million, into Binance.
data.description: Arkham monitoring shows that about seven hours ago, an address starting with "0xE040" deposited 40 million YGG tokens into Binance, worth approximately $2.65 million. This address is not from an ordinary trader. On-chain analysis suggests that the wallet is likely directly linked to the vault of the blockchain game guild Yield Guild Games (YGG), but it is not yet confirmed whether the YGG team's vault will be sold off.

data.title: Bitcoin Market Trends Show Whales Buying During Price Decline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, CryptoQuant founder Ki Young Ju has analyzed the distribution of Bitcoin spot order volumes. The data indicates a pattern during Bitcoin's recent price fluctuations: retail investors tend to buy when prices rise, while whales sell; conversely, when prices fall, retail investors sell and whales buy. The market is currently in a phase where prices are declining, and whales are purchasing.

data.title: Bitcoin saw bear market in 2025, 'decade long' bull run ahead: Mow
data.description:
Bitcoin reached new all-time highs in October, yet Jan3 founder Samson Mow has described the year as a “bear market” and anticipates a major bull run ahead.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-bear-market-bull-decade-ahead-jan3-samson-mow?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Coinbase CEO: The Genius Act will not be amended; this is Coinbase's red line.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated in a post on the X platform that banks are lobbying Congress to amend the Genius Act. Coinbase will not allow any amendments to this act; it is Coinbase's red line, and they will continue to protect the interests of their customers and the cryptocurrency industry. Armstrong added that he personally predicts that in a few years, once banks realize the enormous business opportunities presented by stablecoins, they will change their stance and lobby Congress to allow stablecoins to pay interest and yield. Therefore, their current efforts are completely futile (and unethical), and the innovator's dilemma remains. Currently, the Genius Act prohibits stablecoin issuers from offering stablecoin rewards, but trading platforms like Gemini, Coinbase, and Kraken can, and this is already included in the existing legislation. If traditional bankers amend the act to prohibit such behavior, it will stifle stablecoin innovation.

data.title: JPMorgan Freezes Accounts of Stablecoin Startups Over Risks
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase freezes accounts of stablecoin startups linked to Venezuela due to high-risk factors.
source: https://coincu.com/news/jpmorgan-freezes-stablecoin-accounts/

data.title: Trump Calls for Release of Names in Epstein Case
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump announced on the Truth platform that millions of new pages of information have been discovered in the Epstein case. He stated that the Department of Justice is being forced to dedicate all its time to addressing what he described as a Democrat-driven hoax. Trump instructed the Department of Justice to release the names of all individuals involved.

data.title: Largest U.S. State Pension Fund Expands Exposure to Bitcoin Treasury Firm Strategy
data.description:
The California Public Employees’ Retirement System (CalPERS), the largest U. S. state pension fund, has increased its exposure to Strategy ($MSTR), a company known for holding Bitcoin as a treasury asset. According to the latest disclosure, CalPERS has purchased 183,444 shares, with the position valued at approximately $29 million so far this year
source: https://www.ethnews.com/largest-u-s-state-pension-fund-expands-exposure-to-bitcoin-treasury-firm-strategy/
